sub_pixel_system.notes: Infinity Engine based game; sub-pixel data not documented in available sources

special_mechanics.name: isometric_movement
special_mechanics.description: Click-to-move control scheme in isometric perspective; character moves via point-and-click interaction with environment

special_mechanics.name: d_and_d_ruleset
special_mechanics.description: Movement governed by Advanced Dungeons & Dragons 2nd Edition ruleset; character class affects mobility capabilities

tier_notes: Tier 2: Planescape: Torment is a narrative-driven CRPG using the Infinity Engine. The available Wikipedia source contains no quantitative physics data (movement speeds, gravity values, jump mechanics, sub-pixel systems, acceleration/deceleration rates). As an RPG built on a modified Infinity Engine with click-to-move controls and D&D 2E ruleset, precise physics parameters are not documented in accessible sources. Movement is primarily governed by game rules abstraction rather than frame-by-frame platforming physics.
sources_used: wikipedia: Planescape: Torment article - gameplay section
